meaning: n.枪炮的齐射,猛射,连续射击vt.齐射
examples: He faced a fusillade of questions from the waiting journalists.
One leading theory: it came from a fusillade of comets that came screaming back in toward the sun a half-billion years or so after our planet formed.
Two of Durutte's regiments go and come in affright as though tossed back and forth between the swords of the Uhlans and the fusillade of the brigades of Kempt, Best, Pack, and Rylandt;
Salespeople and big-data enthusiasts, who applauded the concept in a fusillade of Twitter posts.
If Ms Kagan was unsettled by this fusillade she did not betray it.
